name: Siva Kumar — QA Manager
subject: Siva — test maintenance eating sprints at Replicon?
body:
Hi Siva,

Building automated tests for a workforce management platform usually means your test base is constantly patching broken locators when APIs shift. Is that something eating into your team's sprint time?

Medibuddy was dealing with the same maintenance drag and cut it 50% on Testsigma. Their test engineering team stopped spending sprints on locator repair and started writing new coverage.

At Replicon's scale, regression stability compounds across every release cycle. How much of your current sprint capacity is going to test maintenance versus building new coverage?

Cheers,
Rob

body:

Samsung got consistent regression coverage across iOS, Android, and web using Testsigma. No longer debugging different failures across platforms.

Nagra DTV built 2,500 tests in 8 months on Testsigma while managing multiple concurrent platforms. Their team stopped worrying about tooling friction and focused on coverage growth instead.
